terms.definition: A data point that lies unusually far from the bulk of a feature's distribution; may be an error, a rare valid event, or evidence of a sub-population.
Interquartile range: Q3 − Q1, the spread of the middle 50% of sorted data; used in Tukey fences for robust outlier detection.
An outlier boundary at Q1 − k×IQR or Q3 + k×IQR; k = 1.5 is the usual choice for mild outliers, k = 3 for far outliers.
Standardized value z = (x − μ) / σ measuring how many standard deviations x lies from the mean; |z| above a threshold (often 3) flags outliers.
To cap extreme values at chosen lower and upper bounds instead of deleting them; limits influence while keeping sample size.
A summary (median, IQR, MAD) that is not heavily distorted by a small fraction of extreme values, unlike mean and standard deviation.
